notes: 97 territories across the 13 fixed AH areas (~6-9 each). This is the original 96 plus ONE added territory, 'Assyria' (upper-Tigris / Jazira), inserted into middle_east so that the two Mesopotamian empire pairs get distinct starts (Sumer vs Akkad in Epoch I; Babylon vs Assyria in Epoch II). If you must trim toward ~90, the most droppable filler/island spaces remain Crete, Ireland, Shikoku, Ryukyu, Gran Chaco, Low Countries.

Exactly 8 barren (impassable wasteland) territories, matching the AH '8 grey wastelands': Empty Quarter (Rub al Khali), Sahara, Gobi, Siberia (taiga), Greenland (icecap), Amazon (rainforest depths), Antarctica (Antarctic edge), Outback (central Australian desert). The barren flag = game-impassable, NOT vegetation.

Exactly 18 resource territories (strong monument candidates): Mesopotamia, Lower Nile, North China, South China, Indus, Ganges, Italy, Gaul, Indochina, Java, Pontic Steppe, Mississippi, Mexico, Andes, Pampas, Honshu, West Africa, Zimbabwe. The added Assyria territory is NOT a resource (Mesopotamia remains the middle_east river-valley resource).

Great Wall placed on North China's northern steppe frontier (facing Mongolia/Manchuria) - the single great_wall tag. It directly modifies the Mongolia -> North China invasion edge that the Epoch VI Mongols use.

All five prompt-named straits are crossable choke points: Gibraltar (Iberia <-> Mauretania), Bosporus (Balkans/Anatolia, southern_europe <-> middle_east), Dover (Britain <-> Gaul), Malacca (Malaya <-> Sumatra), Bering (Alaska <-> Siberia). Bonus real straits also tagged: Messina (Sicily), Tsushima/Korea (Kyushu <-> Korea), Magellan/Drake (Patagonia <-> Antarctica), Bass/Tasman (Tasmania/New Zealand), Torres (New Guinea <-> Eastern Australia).

Adjacency convention: a territory's neighborsHint lists real-world land neighbors plus annotated sea crossings as 'X (across <strait/sea>)'. Build land edges from the plain names; build sea edges where two territories share a sea id and at least one notes an 'across' link. This seeds a fully connected graph spanning all continents.

Cross-area bridge territories enable historic steppe/empire expansion: Caucasus, Central Asia, Persia connect middle_east<->eurasia<->india/china; the new Assyria bridges Mesopotamia<->Anatolia/Zagros/Caucasus; Pontic Steppe + Balkans connect eurasia<->southern_europe; Anatolia + Levant connect middle_east<->Europe/Africa.

Note: the 'Mongolia' territory is filed under the china area (not eurasia) - that is the geography's grouping choice, and it is the correct historical homeland for the Epoch VI Mongols, who therefore have homelandArea = china. Likewise 'Manchuria' (Qing homeland) sits in the china area.

Seas list is 29 navigable bodies: the prompt-named ids plus the regional seas the geography needs. Caspian is a closed inland sea but kept navigable for steppe-empire amphibious links (Khazars on the Volga/Caspian, Russia's Caspian flotilla).

Every empire homeland maps cleanly onto an existing territory in its homelandArea, and no two empires in the SAME epoch share a territory. Homeland density is highest in middle_east (3 of 7 in both Epoch I and II) - the cradle-of-civilization crowding is intentional.

buildNotes: ROSTER SHAPE: exactly 7 empires per epoch x 7 epochs = 49, every homeland distinct within its epoch. Two empires were cut from the supplied groups to hit 7/epoch (Epoch III had 8, Epoch V had 8): Macedon/Alexander (Epoch III) and the Tibetan Empire (Epoch V). Both are strong add-back candidates for an expanded deck or higher player counts (see openIssues).

GEOGRAPHIC RAMP (core balance lever): Epochs I-II are deliberately confined to the Old-World cradles - every empire sits in middle_east, north_africa, china, india, or southern_europe. Epoch III-IV stay Old-World but add the eurasian steppe (marauder lanes: Goths/Huns). Epoch V is the first to reach sub-Saharan Africa (Ghana -> West Africa). Epoch VI is the first to touch the Americas (Inca -> Andes, Aztec -> Mexico). Epoch VII spans the globe (USA -> Eastern Woodlands plus four all-ocean colonial powers). Place starting-army and VP weight so that nobody can profitably rush the empty Americas/Oceania before Epoch VI.

FRONTIER AREAS WITH NO HOMELANDS: southeast_asia, nippon, and australia hold zero empire homelands by design - they are pure expansion/scoring frontier (matching HotW, where Indonesia/Japan/Oceania are peripheral VP areas reached by sea). Make sure their territories are reachable by the maritime empires (Ming, Tang, British, Spanish, Dutch-style raiders) and the Pacific island chain (Philippines-Borneo-New Guinea-Polynesia) so they are contestable late.

STRENGTH SCALE: strengths use a compressed 3-9 band, NOT the raw Avalon Hill army counts (raw AH ranges ~3 Minoans to ~15 Romans / 13 British). Treat 'strength' as the empire card's army allotment on this normalized scale. Ceiling rises by epoch: I=5, II=6, III=7, IV=8, V=9, VI=9, VII=9; floor also rises: I=3 up to VII=6. Anchors follow the rulebook ratios (Persians mid, Rome/Carthage high-classical, British top). Marauders sit near their epoch ceiling (Huns 6, Vikings 6, Mongols 9) because their card value is raw military power, not territory holding.

DRAW ORDER (catch-up rule): 'order' 1..7 = strength ascending, ties broken by earlier historical start. order 1 = weakest/earliest, drawn FIRST by the trailing player; order 7 = the epoch's strongest, drawn LAST. Use 'order' to sequence the per-epoch draw pile. Per epoch the strongest are: I Akkad(5), II Assyria(6), III Qin/Han(7), IV Imperial Rome(8), V Arab Caliphate(9), VI Mongols(9), VII British(9).

MARAUDERS (hasCapital=false): six cards - Celts (E3, Gaul), Goths (E4, Pontic Steppe) + Huns (E4, Kazakh Steppe), Vikings (E5, Scandinavia), Mongols (E6, Mongolia). They start on steppe/forest spaces adjacent to barren wastes and score by raiding rather than holding a capital. Vikings navigate 'all' seas (longships); steppe marauders are land-only except Goths (Black/Aegean coastal raids). Ensure each marauder homeland touches a barren or an open steppe corridor (Mongolia<->Gobi, Kazakh/Pontic Steppe corridor, Scandinavia<->North Atlantic).

CONNECTIVITY BRIDGES to honor when drawing edges: Caucasus + Persia + Central Asia stitch middle_east<->eurasia<->india/china (Achaemenids, Sassanids, Mongols, Mughals, Russians); Pontic Steppe + Balkans stitch eurasia<->southern_europe (Goths, Huns, Ottomans, Russians); Anatolia + Levant stitch middle_east<->Europe/Africa (Romans, Arabs, Ottomans); the added Assyria bridges Mesopotamia<->Anatolia/Zagros/Caucasus. The Bering strait (Alaska<->Siberia) is the ONLY near-land Old-World<->New-World link; every other Americas approach is ocean (Atlantic for Spain/Britain/France/USA/Vikings-via-Greenland; Pacific edge).

STRAITS & WALL: keep the five named straits as crossable but contestable choke points - Gibraltar, Bosporus, Dover, Malacca, Bering. The single Great Wall sits on North China's northern edge and should give a defense modifier on the Mongolia/Manchuria -> North China edge (used by Mongols E6 and Qing E7), not block it.

BARREN PLACEMENT (8): Empty Quarter, Sahara, Gobi, Siberia, Greenland, Amazon, Antarctica, Outback. They partition the board into the historic Old-World / New-World / Oceania theaters, protect marauder flanks (Gobi behind Mongolia; the open steppe behind Huns/Goths), and keep early epochs from leaking into empty continents.

RESOURCE PLACEMENT (18, prime monument/wonder sites): the great river valleys and wealth zones - Mesopotamia, Lower Nile, North China, South China, Indus, Ganges, Italy, Gaul, Indochina, Java, Pontic Steppe, Mississippi, Mexico, Andes, Pampas, Honshu, West Africa, Zimbabwe. Twelve are Old-World homelands (fuels early-epoch contest in the cradles); Mississippi/Mexico/Andes/Pampas pre-seed the Americas so Epoch VI-VII arrivals have prizes; Java/Honshu/Zimbabwe reward late maritime/peripheral expansion.

HOMELAND DENSITY / ANTI-SNOWBALL: middle_east supplies 3 of 7 homelands in BOTH Epoch I and II (Sumer/Hittites/Akkad, then Babylon/Assyria/Phoenicia) and southern_europe peaks in Epoch III (Greece + Rome). Tune per-territory VP so this cradle crowding does not let an early Fertile-Crescent leader snowball - the AH VP table already pays the small Old-World areas less per territory than the larger continents; preserve that gradient.

AUTHENTIC ROSTER (2026-06-27, docs/AUTHENTIC-RULES.md §11): real 1993 AH names/strengths/marauders/epochs. Start-lands mapped to nearest CURRENT territory (coarse map). FLAGS for the engine phase: Sumeria should be a neutral pre-game SEED not a drafted Epoch-I empire; Inca+Aztec share one Epoch-VI card (only Aztec listed); Spain & Portugal collide on Iberia (board has Western Iberia vs Pyrenees); navigation seas reference current ids (some invented — reconcile to 3 oceans + 10 seas later); intra-epoch `order` is provisional (need exact card numbers).
openIssues: Two empires were cut to reach exactly 7/epoch: Macedon/Alexander (Epoch III, strength 6, homeland Pella -> Balkans) and the Tibetan Empire (Epoch V, strength 5, homeland Lhasa -> Tibet). Re-add either for >7-player variants or an expanded deck; Macedon would make southern_europe Epoch III a 3-empire cluster (Greece+Macedon+Rome), Tibetan would make china Epoch V a 2-empire cluster (Tang+Tibet).

Added one new territory 'Assyria' (upper Tigris / Jazira) to middle_east purely to give distinct homelands to the two same-epoch Mesopotamian pairs. Assyria-the-empire (E2) fits it perfectly; Akkad (E1) is placed there as a mild north-shift of its true central-Mesopotamian core (Agade) for playability. Alternative: drop the new territory and let two E1 empires contest a single Mesopotamia.

Capital-vs-homeland choices where the two diverge: Sassanid Persia started at Persia (Iranian/Fars homeland) though its capital Ctesiphon is in Mesopotamia; Qing started at Manchuria (Manchu homeland) though its capital became Beijing/North China; Ming started at South China (founding capital Nanjing) though it later ruled from Beijing/North China. Swap any of these to capital-location starts if you prefer.

Kushan placed at Punjab and Huns at Kazakh Steppe specifically to keep them distinct from Gupta (Ganges) and Goths (Pontic Steppe) in the same epoch. Both are defensible (Kushan Gandhara/Peshawar core; Hunnic eastern-steppe origin before Pannonia) but are placement-for-distinctness decisions, not the only option.

Mongols' homelandArea was set to china (the area the geography files 'Mongolia' under), correcting the supplied roster's 'eurasia' label. The Mongolia territory is the historically correct homeland; only the area-grouping label changed.

navigationSeas uses snake_case ids matching the seas list; 'all' is a sentinel for global-ocean empires (Vikings, British, Spanish, French) and 'none' = landlocked. If the engine needs an explicit enumerated list instead of 'all', expand it to every sea id at build time.

No additional WebSearch beyond two confirmatory queries was needed: all roster facts (epochs, homelands, capital/marauder status, the compressed strength ramp) were already grounded in the supplied source list, and the merge was a reconcile/placement task rather than new fact-finding.
